items.title: Inertia moves to commercialize one of the world’s most elaborate science experiments
items.summary: Inertia has signed three agreements with the Lawrence Livermore National Lab, paving the way for the company to bring its pioneering fusion reactor to market.

items.title: An Amazon warehouse worker died on the job at Oregon facility
items.summary: Though Amazon has faced issues with warehouse safety, the company said that the death was not work related.

items.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
items.summary: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

items.title: Uber and Nuro begin testing premium robotaxi service in San Francisco
items.summary: Uber employees can now hail a Lucid robotaxi as part of the testing.

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.summary: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connections. The app had already been scheduled for retirement.

items.title: Thousands of rare concert recordings are landing on the Internet Archive — listen now
items.summary: Chicago-based music superfan Aadam Jacobs has been recording the concerts he goes to since the 1980s, amassing an archive of over 10,000 tapes.

items.title: IBM pays $17M fine to end DOJ suit over DEI programs
items.summary: IBM entered into a $17 million settlement agreement on Friday with the U.S. DOJ over allegations that it engaged in “illegal DEI practices.”

items.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
items.summary: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

items.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
items.summary: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.

items.title: FBI announces takedown of phishing operation that targeted thousands of victims
items.summary: Cybercriminals allegedly used the W3LL phishing kit to target more than 17,000 victims worldwide, stealing their passwords and multi-factor authentication codes.

items.title: Booking.com confirms hackers accessed customers’ data
items.summary: The travel giant notified customers that their personal data, including names, email addresses, and phone numbers, may have been accessed in a security incident.

items.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
items.summary: While many startups founded prior to the emergence of ChatGPT are struggling to position themselves for the AI era, Vercel, a 10-year-old dev tool and website hosting platform, is benefiting from the explosion of AI-generated apps and agents.

items.title: Hack at Anodot leaves over a dozen breached companies facing extortion
items.summary: The data breach at Anodot, which affects customers like Rockstar Games, is the latest hack aimed at stealing data from a large number of corporate giants.

items.title: Slate Auto raises $650M to fund its affordable EV truck plans
items.summary: Slate Auto's latest funding round was led by existing investor TWG Global, a firm run by LA Dodgers owner Mark Walter.

items.title: Roblox introduces ‘Kids’ and ‘Select’ accounts for age-appropriate access to games and chat
items.summary: Users aged five to nine will be assigned to a "Roblox Kids" account, and users aged nine to 15 to will be put in a "Roblox Select" account.

items.title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
items.summary: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

items.title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
items.summary: The report is particularly surprising since the Department of Defense recently declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

items.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
items.summary: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

items.title: X says it’s reducing payments to clickbait accounts
items.summary: X is cutting back on payments to accounts that are “flooding the timeline” with clickbait and rapid-fire news aggregation, according to its head of product Nikita Bier.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Who is poaching all the self-driving vehicle talent?
items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.
